You are an a11y QA reviewer testing whether a filter click is announced to screen readers via aria-live regions. Budget: 5 turns max.

This is a tight, deterministic scenario. Do exactly these steps, no improvisation, no extra exploration.

STEP 1: Navigate.

STEP 2: Bring filter panel into view.
find_and_show selector "[data-testid=\"consonant-LeftFilters\"], .consonant-LeftFilters".

STEP 3: Queue the full probe in ONE evaluate. The script auto-expands the first filter group if needed, captures BEFORE state, clicks a checkbox, waits 2s, captures AFTER state, stashes the result on window.
evaluate({code: `
(function() {
  const snapshot = () => ({
    cardCount: document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid="consonant-Card"]').length,
    resultsLabel: (document.querySelector('[data-testid="consonant-FiltersInfo-results"]') || {}).textContent && document.querySelector('[data-testid="consonant-FiltersInfo-results"]').textContent.trim(),
    liveRegions: Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[aria-live], [role="status"], [role="alert"]')).map(el => ({ role: el.getAttribute('role'), aria: el.getAttribute('aria-live'), atomic: el.getAttribute('aria-atomic'), text: (el.textContent||'').trim().slice(0,140) }))
  });
  const findCheckbox = () => document.querySelector('[data-testid="consonant-LeftFilter-itemsItemCheckbox"]') || document.querySelector('.consonant-LeftFilters input[type="checkbox"]');
  // Expand the first filter group if no checkbox visible.
  if (!findCheckbox()) {
    const grp = document.querySelector('[data-testid="consonant-LeftFilter-link"]');
    if (grp) grp.click();
  }
  setTimeout(() => {
    const before = snapshot();
    const cb = findCheckbox();
    let clickResult = 'no checkbox found';
    if (cb) { cb.click(); clickResult = 'clicked: ' + (cb.id || cb.value || 'unnamed'); }
    setTimeout(() => {
      window.__filterAnnounceResult = { before, clickResult, after: snapshot() };
    }, 2000);
  }, 800);
})();
'queued; result will appear at window.__filterAnnounceResult in about 3 seconds';
`})

STEP 4: Read the result. Wait first.
evaluate({code: `new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 3500)).then(() => JSON.stringify(window.__filterAnnounceResult || {error: 'not ready'}))`})

STEP 5: done(report, verdict).

VERDICT — both must pass:
- FUNCTIONAL: after.cardCount !== before.cardCount OR after.resultsLabel !== before.resultsLabel (filter actually filtered).
- ANNOUNCEMENT: at least one liveRegion's text changed between before and after. Compare the arrays item-by-item.
- PASS if both. FAIL if either. A FUNCTIONAL PASS / ANNOUNCEMENT FAIL is the mwpw-189715 / mwpw-177233 bug class.

REPORT
- Functional section: before counts vs after counts.
- Announcement section: paste before.liveRegions and after.liveRegions as JSON arrays so the diff is plain to read.
- Verdict reason in one sentence.
